Most professionals structure slides like journalists.

Build context. Introduce evidence. Reveal the conclusion at the end.

That works for a newspaper. In a boardroom, it costs you the room.

Decision-makers don't sit through your journey. They need the recommendation in the https://t.co/UfuClrRPde

McKinsey calls it the Pyramid Principle.

Lead with the answer. Then prove it.

Every slide has one job before anything else: answer "So what?"

If a slide can't answer that clearly, it doesn't belong in the deck.

The structure that actually closes rooms:

Open with the insight, not the data
Group your arguments in threes, never more
Every argument tied to a traceable source
Final slide: decision required and next step

That's it. Every high-stakes deck that works follows some version of

Here's the real problem.

Nobody teaches this outside MBB. Most professionals absorb it after years of painful partner feedback and late-night revisions.

The analysts who figure it out early don't work harder than everyone else.

They just communicate at a level that makes

This is not a design tool.

It's a thinking tool that happens to produce boardroom-ready slides.

The difference matters 👇

小米这波牛逼啊，一下掏出三个顶级模型！

前几天在 OpenRouter 杀疯了的 Hunter Alpha 就是 MiMo‑V2‑Pro！

MiMo‑V2‑Pro 旗舰模型：
主要在 Agent 能力和代码能力上很强。

MiMo‑V2‑Omni：
多模态感知模型，负责感知环境为 Agent 模型提供信息。

MiMo‑V2‑TTS：
TTS 语音模型负责 Agent 跟人类沟通 https://t.co/s7v0bBW1Fu
